item.title: Dynamic Secret
item.description: Use strace to trace the runtime syscalls of a binary and read the secret it loads from a file.

item.title: HTTP Inputs
item.description: Send a single HTTP OPTIONS request with the exact query param, header, cookie, and body the server expects simultaneously.

item.title: Math Solver
item.description: Automate solving 100 consecutive linear equations within a single HTTP session to unlock the flag.

item.title: RevMe
item.description: Reverse a three-step transformation (reverse + rotate + XOR) applied to a 36-byte target symbol to recover the flag.

item.title: Secure Admin Panel — Stack Canary Leak + ret2win
item.description: Leak the stack canary via a controlled print function, then overflow to overwrite the return address without triggering the canary check.

item.title: SQLi Cards — Union Injection into SQLite
item.description: Union-based SQL injection in a card lookup form to enumerate tables via sqlite_master and extract the flag.

item.title: XSS Escape — Breaking Out of a Script String Context
item.description: Escape a JavaScript string context inside a script tag by injecting a closing script tag that the sanitizer fails to block.

item.title: Baity5 — Binary Exploitation
item.description: Binary exploitation challenge from Territoriale 2025. Static analysis and attack strategy — no full exploit solved during competition.

item.title: Bash Filter Bypass via Unicode Lookalikes
item.description: Bypass a character-level bash filter by substituting ASCII letters with Unicode homoglyphs that the shell normalizes.

item.title: Segnalazione Cinghiali — Union-Based SQLi in Node/Express
item.description: Union-based SQL injection in the report ID parameter of a Node.js/TypeScript Express app to leak the flag from a hidden table.

item.title: Bank Logic Bypass — Scientific Notation Bypasses Integer Validation
item.description: Submit a withdrawal amount in scientific notation to exploit loose type parsing and credit 1 billion to your balance.

item.title: Basic Calculator — Variable Shadowing Bug
item.description: Identify and work around a Python variable shadowing bug where a function definition overwrites a string variable of the same name.

item.title: Blind SQLi Login — Boolean-Based Character Extraction
item.description: Boolean blind SQL injection on a login form to extract a password character by character using HEX comparison.

item.title: Extract HTML Comments
item.description: Use BeautifulSoup to extract HTML comment nodes from a page and reveal the hidden flag.

item.title: Shell Four ORW — Open-Read-Write Shellcode with Seccomp
item.description: Write open-read-write shellcode to exfiltrate /flag.txt when execve is blocked by a seccomp filter.

item.title: Shell One — Minimal Shellcode to Set EAX
item.description: Write minimal x86 shellcode that sets EAX to 0x13371338 to satisfy the binary’s check and unlock execution flow.

item.title: Split — ret2win with ROP pop rdi Gadget
item.description: Classic x86-64 ret2win: overflow the return address, use a pop rdi gadget to pass the /bin/cat flag.txt string as argument, jump to system.

item.title: Unzip Matryoshka — 3000 Nested Zip Files
item.description: Automate extraction of 3000 nested zip files to retrieve the flag from the innermost archive.

item.title: 2048 — Arithmetic Server Bot
item.description: Connect to a server that fires 2049 arithmetic challenges in Italian — SOMMA, DIFFERENZA, PRODOTTO, POTENZA, DIVISIONE_INTERA — and solve each one in under the timeout to receive the flag.

item.title: A Too Small Reminder — Session ID Enumeration
item.description: Register, log in, notice the session_id cookie is a small integer. Brute-force integers upward from 30 until the admin session is hit and the flag appears.

item.title: ASMR — Static XOR Reverse Engineering
item.description: Reverse a static XOR encryption: subtract flag bytes from key bytes position-by-position to recover the plaintext.

item.title: Based and Encoded — Multi-Format Encoding Bot
item.description: A JSON-framed server demands conversions between base64, hex, and binary in both encode and decode directions. Write a bot that parses the Italian operation description and returns the correct transformed value.

item.title: Basic SQLi — Classic OR 1=1 Login Bypass
item.description: Inject ’ OR ‘1’=‘1 into both username and password fields to make the SQL query always true and grab the flag from the response.

item.title: Byte Flag — Flag Hidden in Raw PNG Bytes
item.description: Open the PNG in binary mode, locate the ASCII string ‘flag’ in the raw byte stream, and print every byte after that index — the flag is embedded verbatim in the file’s binary content.

item.title: C-Style Login — PHP Type Juggling Array Bypass
item.description: PHP’s loose comparison treats an array as truthy against any string. Sending password[] as an array in the POST body bypasses the string comparison and grants access.

item.title: Chaos — TCP Payload Reconstruction from PCAP
item.description: Filter a PCAP for TCP packets, decode each packet’s payload from hex, and concatenate them in order — the resulting byte stream contains the flag.

item.title: Click Me — Cookie Integer Forge to Reach Counter Target
item.description: The app tracks clicks with an integer cookie. Skip the clicking by setting ‘cookies’ to 10000000 directly and request the page — the server trusts the cookie value and returns the flag.

item.title: Coffee Hash — Z3 Cyclic Hash Constraint Solving
item.description: Model a cyclic windowed hash as a system of linear equations over integer variables and solve it with Z3 to recover the flag.

item.title: Confuse Me — PHP Magic Hash 0e MD5 Bypass
item.description: PHP’s loose == treats any string starting with ‘0e’ followed by digits as the float 0. Pass a known magic hash input whose MD5 begins with 0e to bypass a hash comparison.

item.title: Cookie Monster — Base64 JSON Cookie Role Elevation
item.description: The session cookie is a URL-encoded base64 of a JSON string like ‘id-role-username’. Decode it, change the role to 0 and username to admin, re-encode, and access the admin page.

item.title: Corrupted Flag — Fix GIF Magic Bytes then Extract Frames
item.description: The GIF has wrong magic bytes at the start. Replace the first 13 bytes with the correct GIF89a header, open the repaired file with Pillow, and iterate through frames saved as WebP to find the flag.

item.title: CrackMat — Z3 Per-Character Quadratic Equations
item.description: Each flag character satisfies an independent quadratic equation in integer form; Z3 solves all 20 simultaneously to recover the flag.

item.title: Dashed — Six-Layer Encoding Chain: Morse → Hex → Binary → Base64 → Caesar
item.description: Decode a Morse code file, strip hex prefixes, convert to binary, interpret as Base64, then apply a Caesar shift derived from the first character — five transforms chained together.

item.title: Doge Ransom — IBAN Buffer Overflow with Control Byte Injection
item.description: The IBAN input field copies 28 bytes into a buffer with room for 49. Appending the control byte 0x03 after the valid IBAN data overflows into an adjacent flag variable and unlocks the ransomware payment path.

item.title: Doge Ransom 2 — ROP ret2puts Leak then ADMIN re-login
item.description: Overflow the IBAN field to leak the binary’s own password via puts, then re-login as ADMIN using the leaked credential and repeat the overflow to reach the flag path.

item.title: Emergency Call — ROP Syscall Chain for execve
item.description: Write /bin/sh into a known writable address, then build a ROP chain that sets rax=59, rdi=/bin/sh, rsi=0, rdx=0 using dedicated pop gadgets and a syscall instruction to get a shell.

item.title: Flags Shop — Price Parameter Tampering
item.description: The buy endpoint accepts a ‘costo’ (price) POST parameter from the client. Sending costo=0 purchases the expensive flag item for free.

item.title: Formatted — Format String Write with %n
item.description: Use a format string %n write to overwrite a target variable at a known address and unlock the flag path.

item.title: Generatore Poco Casuale — Shellcode Injection via Leaked Stack Address
item.description: The binary leaks a runtime stack address disguised as a ‘random number’. Add 6 to land inside the shellcode region, then spray that address 800 times to cover the return target and get a shell.

item.title: Guess The Number — Stack Overflow + Integer Overflow
item.description: Overflow the name buffer to corrupt the internal number variable, then feed back the overflowed value as a decimal integer to satisfy the equality check.

item.title: Guess the Number 2 — ROP Chain GOT Overwrite via gets
item.description: Build a ROP chain that calls gets twice to plant arbitrary data in the GOT, redirect strcspn to a shellcode stub, then trigger the win print path.

item.title: Headache — Flag in HTTP Response Header
item.description: The flag is not in the response body — it is stored in a custom HTTP response header called ‘Flag’. Use a HEAD request and read r.headers[‘Flag’].

item.title: I Got Magic — GIF Polyglot Webshell Upload + RCE
item.description: Craft a file that is simultaneously a valid GIF (magic bytes GIF89a) and a PHP shell (<?php echo system('cat /flag.txt'); ?>). Upload it via the image upload form, find the timestamped filename in the response, and request that URL to execute the shell.

item.title: Just a Reminder — Obfuscated JS Secret Key Recovery
item.description: The login form runs client-side JS. Deobfuscating or inspecting it reveals a hardcoded secret key used to AES-decrypt the flag — the key is plaintext at the top of the obfuscated script.

item.title: Light or Dark — Path Traversal with Dot Obfuscation + Null Byte
item.description: The theme parameter appends .css to the user-supplied path before serving it. Use …/ triples (which reduce to ../) and a URL-encoded null byte to escape the CSS directory and read /flag.txt.

item.title: Make a Wish — PHP GET Array Type Coercion Bypass
item.description: Pass the GET parameter as an array (?richiesta[]=sas) to make PHP receive an array instead of a string, bypassing the string equality check and revealing the flag.

item.title: More Private Club — Simple ret2win Buffer Overflow
item.description: Overflow a stack buffer to overwrite the return address with a known win function address, then trigger it.

item.title: No Robots Here — Disallowed Path Discovery via robots.txt
item.description: robots.txt lists a Disallow path that the crawler is never supposed to visit. That path contains the flag.

item.title: No Time — UNION SQL Injection with Keyword Filter Bypass via OFFSET Injection
item.description: A WAF strips forbidden SQL keywords. Inject OFFSET inside reserved words (SELECT → SELOFFSETECT) so after the WAF removes OFFSET the original keyword is reconstructed, landing a UNION SELECT to dump the flag.

item.title: Password Changer 3000 — Insecure Token via Base64-Encoded Username
item.description: The password-reset token is simply the base64 encoding of the username. Encoding ‘admin’ and passing it as the token query parameter triggers the admin password change flow and reveals the flag.

item.title: Quantum Transport Layer — TLS ALPN Flag via gnutls-cli
item.description: The TLS server returns the flag as the negotiated ALPN protocol name. Inject the server’s hostname into /etc/hosts, connect with gnutls-cli specifying –alpn=flag, and read the flag from the TLS handshake output.

item.title: readdle — Two-Stage Shellcode via Stub Read Gadget
item.description: Inject a 4-byte stub shellcode that calls read to pull a full shell payload into the same buffer, then jump to it — bypassing the initial 4-byte size constraint on the first write.

item.title: Rick Roller — Flag Behind a Redirect
item.description: The /get_flag.php endpoint immediately redirects the browser to a Rick Astley video. Disabling redirect following in requests reveals the 302 response body which contains the flag.

item.title: Secret Vault — Heap Address Leak + Shellcode via Stack Overflow
item.description: Trigger a heap allocation that the binary prints, compute the shellcode landing address at heap+96, then overflow the stack with that return address followed by shellcode to get a shell.

item.title: Shell's Revenge — GIF Polyglot PHP Webshell Upload
item.description: Upload a file starting with the GIF89a magic bytes followed by a PHP shell. The server validates MIME type from the header but saves the file where PHP can execute it. Access the uploaded file URL to run arbitrary commands.

item.title: Shell's Revenge 2 — GIF Polyglot Webshell via LFI Include
item.description: Upload a GIF polyglot containing a PHP shell, then trigger its execution through a local file inclusion vulnerable ?page= parameter that includes the uploaded file path.

item.title: Sito Vuoto — Flag Hidden in Page Source
item.description: The homepage appears empty, but the flag is embedded in an HTML comment or inside one of the linked static files (CSS or JS). Fetch each resource and grep for the flag pattern.

item.title: Sn4ck Sh3nan1gans — UNION SQL Injection via Base64 JSON Cookie
item.description: The server reads a base64-encoded JSON cookie containing an ID field and passes it unsanitised into a SQL query. Inject a UNION SELECT payload inside the JSON, re-encode as base64, and set the forged cookie to extract the flag in three phases.

item.title: Sniff N Byte — Decode Hardcoded Hex Flag
item.description: The flag is encoded as concatenated 0x-prefixed hex bytes embedded directly in the capture. Strip the prefixes, decode with bytes.fromhex, and print.

item.title: SSA0x42 — XOR Key Recovery from Known-Plaintext PCAP Headers
item.description: Two known-plaintext byte sequences (k and l) from the PCAP header XOR to reveal the repeating key. XOR the encrypted flag block with that key to recover the plaintext.

item.title: Suoni Misteriosi — Morse Audio Decoder
item.description: A WAV file carries the flag in Morse code audio. Decode it automatically with morse-audio-decoder and wrap the result in flag{…}.

item.title: Super Market — Integer Underflow Price Manipulation
item.description: Pass a negative quantity to an unsigned arithmetic check, causing integer underflow that bypasses a balance validation and grants access to a premium item.

item.title: Terminator — Canary Leak + Full ret2libc
item.description: Overwrite the canary’s null byte to leak the full 8-byte cookie over printf, simultaneously leak a saved RBP to base the stack, then use puts@got to find libc base and call system(’/bin/sh’) — all in two trips through the same vulnerable function.

item.title: That's a Lot of Fs — Flag in Ethernet Destination MAC via Custom EtherType
item.description: Filter Ethernet frames with EtherType 0xffff — the custom protocol used by this challenge — collect the destination MAC address from each matching frame, interpret the first two hex bytes as ASCII, and concatenate to reveal the flag.

item.title: Time Is Key — Timing Side-Channel Flag Extraction
item.description: The server compares the submitted flag character-by-character and returns a response proportional to how many correct characters were prefixed. Measure elapsed time per candidate character and pick the one with the longest response time to recover the flag byte by byte.

item.title: TIMP — OS Command Injection with IFS and Null Byte Filter Bypass
item.description: A POST endpoint executes a shell command with user-supplied input but filters spaces, the word ’echo’, and other shell metacharacters. Bypass with ${NULL} (strips to nothing) and ${IFS} (the internal field separator, expands to a space in bash).

item.title: Truly Random Signature — Predictable Session Token Analysis
item.description: The server issues session tokens that are predictable or reusable. Requesting the site twice, comparing the Set-Cookie headers, and identifying the pattern allows forging or reusing an admin-level token.

item.title: Useless — Flag Hidden in PCAPNG via strings
item.description: Run strings on the PCAPNG and grep for ‘flag’ — the flag is stored as plain ASCII inside the capture file and visible without any packet parsing.

item.title: Villa Pisani — DNS Maze DFS via CNAME Records
item.description: Navigate a DNS-based labyrinth by following CNAME records in four compass directions (up/down/left/right) and reading TXT records until one contains the flag. Depth-first search with a visited set prevents loops.

item.title: WordWang — Protocol Format Wrapping
item.description: The server returns a word from a custom protocol. Wrap the response in the expected format — prepend ‘?’, uppercase, append ‘!’ — send it back, and the server returns the flag.

item.title: You Complete Me — Binary Search Word Reconstruction
item.description: A sorted word list and a known sequence of binary-search response sizes pin down a unique word character by character. Reconstruct it by matching the precounted word counts at each prefix level.

item.title: Zipception — 3000 Nested ZIPs
item.description: A flag is hidden inside 3000 recursively nested ZIP archives. Loop backwards from flag3000.zip to flag1.zip, extracting and deleting each archive in turn until the innermost file is revealed.

item.title: Zipception 2.0 — Nested ZIPs with Password Protection
item.description: 100 nested password-protected ZIPs. Crack each archive’s password against the rockyou wordlist, extract, delete, and repeat — 100 times.
